preamble: The shift from 'e' to 'o' involves backing and rounding, often triggered by surrounding consonants or morphological processes.

examples.note: Shift of stressed 'e' to 'o' (yo) after palatalized consonants.

sources: Beekes, R. S. P. (2011). Comparative Indo-European Linguistics.
Comrie, B. (1981). The Languages of the Soviet Union.
